pySEs documentation¶

  • Usage
    • Installation
    • Run an idealized test
  • Quickstart
    • Imports
    • Vertical grid data (CAM-30 hybrid coefficients)
    • Example 1 – Quasi-uniform grid, steady-state check
    • Example 2 – Stretched grid, baroclinic wave with topography
    • Key concepts
  • The pySEs public API
    • pyses.grids — Horizontal grid generation
      • init — grid constructors
      • parallelism — distributed-memory helpers
    • pyses.initialize — Initial conditions
      • ullrich_baroclinic_wave — DCMIP 2016 moist baroclinic wave
      • custom_init — low-level initialisation primitives
    • pyses.model_utils — Model configuration and utilities
      • model_info — model identifiers
      • model_config — physics and numerics configuration
      • mass_coordinate — vertical grid utilities
      • model_state — state arithmetic and remapping
      • parallel_utils — array device helpers
      • operators — 3-D differential operators
    • pyses.simulate — Running the dynamical core
    • pyses.shallow_water — Shallow-water models
      • williamson_init — Williamson test cases
      • galewsky_init — Galewsky jet test case
  • How is the atmospheric state represented in pySEs?
    • state["dynamics"] — prognostic fields
      • Always-present fields
      • Thermodynamic variable
      • Non-hydrostatic-only fields
    • state["static_forcing"] — time-invariant forcing
      • Always-present fields
      • Conditionally-present fields
    • state["tracers"] — moisture and passive tracers
      • Overview
      • "moisture_species"
      • "tracers"
      • "dry_air_species"
      • Mixing-ratio convention flag
  • Horizontal grid generation
  • Contributing
    • Coding Style
    • How do we make an accessible, maintainable, friendly dycore?
    • Dependencies
    • Quickstarting your Fortran Port
      • Step 1: Unperformant Literal Translation
      • Step 2: Write tests
      • Step 3: Write a more Numpy+Pythonic function
